name: Legal's Trap
description: White sacrifices the queen with Nxe5! If Black greedily takes the queen (Bxd1?), White plays Bxf7+ forcing the king to e7, then Nd5 delivers checkmate. The king is boxed in by its own pieces: the pawn on d6, the queen on d8, the bishop on f8, and White's bishop on f7.

name: Fried Liver Attack
description: White sacrifices the knight on f7 with Nxf7!, forcing the Black king into the centre. The king on f7 is exposed to a devastating queen check on f3, and White has a ferocious attack that Black has no practical defence against over the board.

name: Noah's Ark Trap
description: Black lures White's bishop to a4/b3 and then advances queenside pawns — a6, b5, c5, and finally c4 — to trap it with no escape squares. The bishop is buried alive like Noah's ark under the flood of pawns.

name: Fishing Pole Trap
description: Black 'dangles' the knight on g4 as bait. After h3, Black plays h5! — offering the rook as a decoy. If White captures hxg4, Black plays hxg4 and the knight on f3 has nowhere safe to go. The attack with Qh4 and g3 becomes overwhelming.

name: Blackburne Shilling Gambit
description: Black sacrifices the e5 pawn and plays the surprising Nd4! — a knight to the centre that is immediately attacked but cannot safely be taken. After White captures with Nxe5, Black strikes with Qg5!, threatening both Qxg2 (winning the rook) and Nxf3+ (forking king and queen).

name: Siberian Trap
description: Black sets a sophisticated ambush: after White castles, Black plays Ng4! attacking the queen and threatening the deadly Nxf2 fork (winning the exchange). White's natural queen move to e2 drops into the trap — the queen has no good square after Ng4.

name: Magnus Smith Trap
description: After Black plays g6 (heading for the Dragon), White springs a surprise: Nxc6! followed by e5, blasting open the centre before Black has completed development. Black's doubled pawns on c6 and the exposed king on e8 leave Black in serious trouble.

name: Marshall Trap
description: After a long manoeuvring sequence, Black sacrifices the bishop on h2 — Bxh2+! — to expose the White king. The rook on e1 is pinned by Black's bishop, making the king's defence collapse. This elegant combination wins material by force.

name: Stafford Gambit Trap
description: Black declines the symmetrical Petrov and instead gambits by allowing the knight trade on c6. White gains a pawn but falls behind in development. Black's pieces pour out rapidly, and White's natural defensive moves walk into Ng4-Nxf2 forks or a queen trap on h2.

name: Lasker Trap
description: One of the most surprising traps in chess. Black's pawn reaches e3 and if White plays fxe3, Black under-promotes to a knight on g1 — fxg1=N+! — forking the king and rook. White must lose the exchange at minimum.

name: Monticelli Trap
description: Black's knight manoeuvre Ne4-Nxc3 appears to win a piece, but White springs Ng5! threatening Nxf7 — a fork of Black's queen and rook. Black cannot defend both threats and loses material despite apparently being ahead.

name: Elephant Trap
description: Black plays Ne4! and after the exchange on e7 and cxd5, White greedily grabs the d5-pawn with Nxd5?? Black replies with Nxf2!! — a shocking knight sacrifice that forks the White queen and rook. White must give up the queen or suffer a losing attack.

name: Englund Gambit Trap
description: Black immediately gambits a pawn to unbalance the position. After Nc6, Qe7, and the forcing sequence f6/exf6/Nxf6, White's queen on d5 overextends and cannot escape. Black wins material with a fork or positional domination.

name: Caro-Kann Exchange Trap
description: After White plays cxd5 opening the centre, Black's knight on c6 greedily captures the d4 pawn with Nxd4?? — apparently winning a free pawn. But White's queen is perfectly placed to recapture: Qxd4 wins the knight, leaving Black a piece down.
